headline: Corning Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Stock Is Tumbling.
summary: Corning  reported better-than-expected quarterly results but issued a middling outlook for the current quarter, halting the recent rally in optical communications stocks.  Corning stock was up 92% this year as of the close of trading Monday.  This optical communications segment, which has become its largest business, generated net sales of $1.85 billion in the first quarter, up 36% from a year ago.

headline: Corning faces consumer electronics drag even as data-center business booms
summary: Corning forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, as weakness in the consumer electronics ‌market continues to pressure business despite strong demand for ‌data center products. Shares of the Gorilla Glass maker fell more than 10% in premarket ​trading. Slower replacement cycles for electronics and cautious consumer spending amid persistent economic uncertainty have pressured Corning's business, offsetting gains in its optical communications segment.

raw.headline: Corning faces consumer electronics drag even as data-center business booms

Shares of the Gorilla Glass maker fell more than 10% in premarket ​trading. Slower replacement cycles for electronics and cautious consumer spending amid persistent economic uncertainty have pressured Corning's business, offsetting gains in its optical communications segment.

headline: Corning Stock Falls Sharply. An Earnings Beat Wasn’t Enough for Investors.
summary: Corning  stock fell sharply Tuesday after the glass and fiber maker reported better-than-expected quarterly results but issued a middling outlook for the current quarter.  While the earnings outlook was in line with Wall Street’s estimates, analysts were looking for sales of $4.65 billion.  Shares tumbled 10% in premarket trading.

headline: Corning (NYSE:GLW) Misses Q1 CY2026 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops
summary: Glass and electronic component manufacturer Corning (NYSE:GLW) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 12.6% year on year to $4.14 billion. Next quarter’s revenue guidance of $4.6 billion underwhelmed, coming in 2.2% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.70 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.
